preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 76%, a commanding edge over Austria's 4% win probability. This 72-percentage-point gap reflects a heavily lopsided forecast. Austria carries minimal theoretical chance of victory according to the statistical approach.

The model's spread reveals a steep asymmetry: Argentina's three-quarter probability contrasts sharply with Austria's near-zero winning chance, while the draw sits at 20%. This configuration suggests Argentina enters as strong favorite, though the 20% draw likelihood indicates the match retains meaningful uncertainty rather than appearing a fait accompli.

The model and sportsbooks diverge substantially. Books strip to 58% Argentina, 25% draw, 18% Austria—more than 18 percentage points lower for Argentina than the model. Polymarket prices align closer to the books. The dashboard presents the model's probabilistic view without asserting which source better predicts the outcome; bettors and analysts should weigh the divergence themselves.
